titel: Stricter rules regarding deportation due to criminal offenses.
html:
The proposal includes suggestions that would allow for more decisions to deport foreign nationals who commit crimes. The proposals include:

  • Crimes will, as a general rule, be grounds for deportation when the penalty imposed is stricter than a fine.
  • The weighing of reasons for and against deportation will, in more cases, result in deportation.
  • The strict requirements for deporting foreign nationals who came to Sweden before the age of 15 will be removed.
  • Enforcement obstacles will no longer be considered by the district court when deciding whether or not to order deportation.
  • Re-entry bans will become longer and, in more cases, without a time limit.
  • Prosecutors will have a duty to request deportation when a foreign national is charged with crimes that are grounds for deportation.

The proposal also includes, among other things, stricter penalties for certain offenses under the Aliens Act.

The proposed amendments are to come into force on September 1, 2026.

Proposed Parliamentary Decision

The government's proposal: The Riksdag (Swedish Parliament) adopts the government's proposal for an amendment to the Aliens Act (2005:716). 4
tldr: It will become easier to deport foreign nationals who commit crimes. As a general rule, any penalty that is stricter than a fine should be grounds for deportation. Re-entry bans will be extended, sometimes without a time limit, and prosecutors will be obligated to request deportation when someone is charged with a crime that constitutes grounds for deportation.
lattlast:
The government wants to make it easier to make decisions to deport foreign nationals who commit crimes.

As a general rule, a crime should be grounds for deportation even when the penalty is more severe than a fine. The balance between the arguments for and against deportation should more often result in deportation, and the special requirements for deporting individuals who came to Sweden before the age of 15 will be removed.

Deportation bans will become longer and, in more cases, will have no time limit. Prosecutors will also be required to request deportation when a foreign national is charged with a crime that could lead to deportation.
